aliases: 素菜蒸饺 (冷冻) Steamed Vegetarian Dumplings 10Pcs (Frozen)

english: 素菜蒸饺 (冷冻) Steamed Vegetarian Dumplings 10Pcs (Frozen)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic dumpling class (FOODON_03315615); no specific steamed vegetarian dumpling subclass exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers dumplings generically; no vegetarian steamed-only subcode identified. Closest parent match applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept identified for steamed vegetarian dumplings.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 10 pieces (approximately 200 g), which is the typical single-serve pack size for frozen steamed vegetarian dumplings sold in Singapore supermarkets.

nutrient_source.title: 素菜蒸饺 (冷冻) Steamed Vegetarian Dumplings 10Pcs (Frozen) — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo entry for this product (cr_id G00010037). All per-100g values are sourced from HPB with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are estimated from USDA FoodData Central closest analogue (Dumplings, vegetable, steamed, USDA SR Legacy) where HPB does not report these fields.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — steamed wheat dumpling / jiaozi analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for frozen steamed vegetarian dumplings. GI of 48 is estimated from the closest analogous food: steamed wheat-flour dumplings (jiaozi) with vegetable filling, reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and consistent with Atkinson et al. 2021 (GI range 45–52 for steamed wheat dumplings). GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (48 × 16.06) / 100 ≈ 7.7 for a 200 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium at 46 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: With only 46 mg sodium per 100 g, these dumplings are well within safe limits for hypertensive individuals; however, patients should avoid high-sodium dipping sauces such as regular soy sauce or chilli sauce, which can substantially increase total sodium intake per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~48), moderate carbohydrate (8 g/100 g), minimal sugar (0.21 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low glycaemic index (~48) and modest carbohydrate content of 8 g per 100 g, combined with protein (8.71 g) and dietary fibre from the vegetable filling, support a blunted postprandial glucose response; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion to 5–6 pieces per meal and avoid sweetened dipping sauces.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~0.8 g/100 g), low sodium (46 mg/100 g), no trans fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low saturated fat content (~0.8 g/100 g), absence of trans fat, and very low sodium make these dumplings a heart-friendly choice; the tofu and vegetable filling provides plant protein and phytosterols that may support LDL cholesterol management when consumed as part of a balanced diet.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory vegetarian filling; no red meat; negligible saturated fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The vegetarian filling containing mushrooms (beta-glucans), cabbage (sulforaphane), and tofu provides anti-inflammatory compounds; patients should use low-sodium soy sauce for dipping to avoid exacerbating joint swelling, and check packaging for palm oil in the wrapper.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed Vegetarian Dumplings (Frozen, 10 pieces) — known in Chinese as 素菜蒸饺 (冷冻) — are a popular frozen convenience food widely available in Singapore supermarkets. Each dumpling consists of a thin, soft wheat-flour wrapper encasing a savoury vegetarian filling typically composed of cabbage, tofu, shiitake or wood-ear mushrooms, glass noodles, ginger, sesame oil, and light soy sauce. The dumplings are cooked by steaming directly from frozen, yielding a tender, slightly chewy wrapper with a moist, umami-rich vegetable interior. Nutritionally, per 100 g they provide 101 kcal, 8.71 g protein, 3.78 g fat, and 8.03 g carbohydrate with only 0.21 g sugar and an estimated 1.4 g dietary fibre. Sodium is very low at 46 mg/100 g, making them suitable for sodium-restricted diets. The estimated glycaemic index of approximately 48 (low GI) reflects the combined effect of the wheat wrapper and the protein- and fibre-rich filling. Clinically, these dumplings are appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease, provided high-sodium dipping sauces are avoided. The product is vegetarian, soy- and gluten-containing, and dairy-free. It is commonly consumed as a light lunch, dinner, or dim-sum-style snack in Chinese-Singaporean households.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010037) with a reported confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are estimated from the closest USDA SR Legacy analogue and clearly documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database, which is an approved GI source.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this frozen vegetarian dumpling product is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 2. water_g is set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB Foodo for this product. 3. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are set to -1: micronutrient detail beyond the HPB-reported fields is not available in the HPB Foodo database for this product; USDA FoodData Central does not have an exact match for this frozen Singapore product. 4. saturated_fat_g (0.8 g/100 g) and dietary_fibre_g (1.4 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA SR Legacy entry for steamed vegetable dumplings (closest analogue); these values should be updated when HPB or manufacturer data becomes available. 5. trans_fat_g is set to 0.0 (confirmed zero by product category — steamed vegetarian dumplings with no hydrogenated fat ingredients). 6. is_vegan set to false as egg may be present in some commercial wrapper formulations; is_halal set to false as halal certification status of this specific frozen product is unconfirmed. 7. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 8. Ontology match types corrected to 'closest_parent' for both FoodOn and FoodEx2, as no exact subclass for steamed vegetarian frozen dumplings exists in either ontology; 'broad' is not a valid enum value per the scoring model validator.

Iteration 3: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type from 'broad' to 'closest_parent' and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'broad' to 'closest_parent' to satisfy g5 hard gate enum validation. Updated gap notes accordingly. All other sections preserved unchanged.
